title: INTERNATIONAL INCENTIVE PROJECT DIRECTOR
description:
At **egg** we make experiences. Real ones.


The kind that take creativity, sharp thinking, attention to detail and, every now and then, a sense of humour when everything changes five minutes before launch.


We work across countries, cultures and clients, so things move fast and no two days look the same. That's part of what we enjoy. It also means we need people who can stay calm under pressure, take ownership and care about getting things right.


We laugh a lot too. Sometimes at work. Mostly at ourselves.


At **egg**, we care about each other as much as we care about our clients and our business. We're collaborative, curious and straightforward. We help each other out when things get busy, we challenge each other to think differently, and we don't leave problems for someone else to solve.


No big egos. No hiding mistakes. No silent chickens in this nest.

**Which brings us to you**


You don't just organise incentive programmes. You create experiences people talk about for years.


You're passionate about travel, naturally curious about the world and always looking for the next destination, hidden gem or unforgettable experience. You understand that the smallest details often have the biggest impact, and you thrive on turning ambitious briefs into seamless, world\-class programmes.


You combine creativity with operational excellence, commercial awareness and calm decision\-making. Whether you're presenting to a client, leading a project team or managing multiple programmes across different countries, you stay focused, adaptable and committed to delivering exceptional work.


Most importantly, you're someone people trust, whether they're clients, colleagues or partners on the other side of the world.

**What you’ll be doing**

* Lead the end\-to\-end delivery of complex international incentive travel programmes, from brief through to onsite execution and post\-event evaluation
* Build trusted relationships with global clients, suppliers and destination partners, acting as a strategic advisor throughout the programme lifecycle
* Develop creative destination concepts and present compelling solutions that exceed client expectations
* Lead cross\-functional project teams, providing direction, coaching and quality oversight while ensuring programmes are delivered on time, on budget and to the highest standard
* Own programme budgets, commercial performance and profitability, balancing exceptional experiences with commercial success
* Travel internationally to lead site inspections, oversee programme delivery and stay ahead of industry trends, emerging destinations and new opportunities

**What you’ll bring**

*(We really mean it. Candidates who do not meet the following criteria will not be considered.)*

**Experience \& skills**

* 10\+ years' experience delivering international incentive travel programmes **within an agency environment**
* Proven experience managing complex, **multi\-country incentive programmes** for global clients
* Strong commercial acumen, including budget ownership and financial management
* Ability to build trusted relationships with clients, suppliers, DMCs and destination partners across international markets
* Exceptional project leadership and organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple programmes simultaneously
* Passion for travel, destinations and creating unforgettable participant experiences
* Curious about AI and how technology can improve the way we create proposals, manage projects and deliver programmes
* **Billingual in English and French** (No scrambled oeufs at **egg**, svp.)

**How we work at egg**


Things move fast here. Priorities shift. Clients expect excellence.


The people who thrive at **egg** tend to:

* Take ownership and get things done
* Stay calm when priorities change
* Focus on outcomes and quality
* Be comfortable with ambiguity
* Look for smarter ways of working, including AI and technology
* Balance creativity with structure and execution

**Bonus points if...**

* You speak additional languages
* You've used AI to improve creativity, efficiency or project delivery
* You have an established international network of suppliers, DMCs or destination partners
* You enjoy discovering emerging destinations and travel trends before everyone else

**What we bring to the table**

* Flexible working setup (remote in some places, hybrid in others)
* Competitive salary \+ performance\-based bonus
* Home office / tech stipend, lunch vouchers
* Smart, supportive colleagues who genuinely enjoy working together
* International career opportunities \+ annual global offsite in somewhere excellent
* Free coffee, pet\-friendly offices and a culture that cares about people as much as performance

*(Yes, the coffee is good. We're not monsters.)*

**The hiring process**

* Online application *(you’ve swiped right)*
* First intro call *(we’ve swiped right too — let’s see if there’s chemistry)*
* First interview *(time to crack things open a bit)*
* Second interview *(meet the family — don’t worry, they’re mostly well\-behaved eggs)*
* Offer *(we’re in love. hope you are too)*

**The most important bit**


At **egg**, feedback goes in all directions: up, down and sideways. We believe accountability and kindness can coexist.


It’s okay to make mistakes here, as long as we own them, learn from them, and don’t keep serving them sunny\-side up forever.


We’re proud of a team made up of people from all walks of life, backgrounds and perspectives. We believe this makes our work better, our thinking sharper, and our outcomes stronger.


We welcome applications from all genders, including transgender and non\-binary people, as well as BIPOC communities, LGBTQ2S\+ communities, and people with visible and non\-visible disabilities.


We are also an ESG\-driven company committed to making a positive impact beyond our own walls.

title: Student Job Customer Service
description:
#### **Intro**


Vaigo is the leading SaaS platform that simplifies and automates corporate mobility.  

We empower large enterprises to manage their mobility policies, budgets, and incentives in one place — with a strong focus on **sustainability, transparency, and efficiency**.  

  

We’re a fast‑growing scale‑up working with leading organizations in Belgium. We only bring in interns who **demonstrate exceptional drive and achievements**.

#### **Your role**


With the Vaigo platform, we make sustainable and flexible mobility easy for companies. What will you be doing concretely?

  

* Managing the administrative processes related to the use of the platform.
* Requesting and cancelling mobility cards and subscriptions with mobility providers.
* Validating registrations in our platform.
* Answering questions from customers and end users in a clear and accurate way.

#### **This is what you are bringing**

* You are fluent in Dutch (French is a plus!).
* You are a student and available for approximately 12 hours per week.
* You hold a Belgian passport.
* You are customer\-oriented, know how to set priorities, and can solve problems effectively.
* You are detail\-oriented and enjoy keeping administration in perfect order.
* You have a hands‑on mentality and a bias for action.
* You demonstrate acumen and ability in AI usage and tooling — you can show clear examples of how you use AI to work extremely efficiently.

#### **This is what you can expect from us**


At Vaigo, you work in a dynamic and fast\-growing scale\-up. Our enthusiastic team of 15 colleagues will welcome you with open arms at our office in Leuven. This role can also be done fully remote. What else can you expect?

  

* A 3 month contract with an hourly wage of €13\.
* Flexibility in working hours, locations and office days.
* Regular team\-building activities.

#### **Proces**

* Send us your application (see below).
* If your profile meets our high standards, we schedule a short interview with our customer operations lead.
* You join and start contributing immediately to meaningful projects.

#### **About us**


Vaigo is the **leading SaaS platform** for corporate mobility, empowering **HR teams** to **simplify and enforce smart mobility policies**. Our all\-in\-one solution ensures employees can access, order, and activate mobility benefits, while HR gains full control over compliance, sustainability, and cost efficiency.  

We partner with large enterprises to transform employee commuting habits, **reducing administrative burden, promoting greener choices, and optimizing mobility spend**.  

Join us in shaping **smarter, greener, and simpler mobility for enterprises**.

title: Senior Finance Analyst (FP&A & Controlling)
description:
**Multiverse Computing**


Multiverse is a well\-funded, fast\-growing deep\-tech company founded in 2019\. We are the largest quantum software company in the EU and have been recognized by CB Insights (2023 and 2025\) as one of the 100 most promising AI companies in the world.
  

With 180\+ employees and growing, our team is fully multicultural and international. We deliver hyper\-efficient software for companies seeking a competitive edge through quantum computing and artificial intelligence.
  

Our flagship products, CompactifAI and Singularity, address critical needs across various industries:

* CompactifAI is a groundbreaking compression tool for foundational AI models based on Tensor Networks. It enables the compression of large AI systems—such as language models—to make them significantly more efficient and portable.
* Singularity is a quantum\- and quantum\-inspired optimization platform used by blue\-chip companies to solve complex problems in finance, energy, manufacturing, and beyond. It integrates seamlessly with existing systems and delivers immediate performance gains on classical and quantum hardware.

You’ll be working alongside world\-leading experts to develop solutions that tackle real\-world challenges. We’re looking for passionate individuals eager to grow in an ethics\-driven environment that values sustainability and diversity.
  

We’re committed to building a truly inclusive culture \- come and join us.  

  



##### **As a Senior Finance Analyst / Controller, you will:**

**Planning \& Analysis**

* Provide financial and business analysis, even when the information available isn't always complete,
* Own project finance, budgeting and profitability tracking,
* Monitor cost center expenses vs. forecast,
* Build reports that turn financial data into business insights,
* Support Budgeting and forecasting,
**Processes \& Controls**

* Collaborate on efficient processes, accounting methods, controls and industry best practices,
* Identify and execute opportunities to automate and standardize processes,
* Establish controls to guarantee data accuracy,
* Control and follow up on posting of supplier invoices, taxes, payroll expenses and accruals,
* Get involved in ERP or systems improvement projects
**Collaboration**

* Coordinate financial closings \& treasury,
* Handle intercompany reconciliations,
* Support external audit processes,
* Guide and mentor junior team members,
* Help lead and grow a small team over time
##### **Required Qualifications**

**Education \& Background**

* A bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field
* 5\-6 years of relevant experience
* Proven experience working in a big four or similar,
* Comfortable working in multi\-entity, multi\-currency environments
**Technical Skills**

* Sharp analytical skills and strong attention to detail
* Hands\-on experience with financial modeling, P\&L management and analysis,
* Expert in MS Office, confident handling large datasets,
* Familiarity with an ERP system \- SB4H is a plus
* Fluent English
**Attitude**

* Self\-starter with a proactive, can\-do attitude,
* Adaptable and comfortable working in a dynamic, fast\-paced environment,
* Comfortable communicating financial insights to non\-financial stakeholders
##### **Perks \& Benefits**

* Indefinite contract.
* Equal pay guaranteed.
* Signing bonus
* Relocation package (if applicable).
* Private health insurance.
* Eligibility for educational budget according to internal policy.
* Hybrid opportunity.
* Flexible working hours.
* Flexible remuneration (Employees in Spain only): hospitality and public transportation
* Language classes and discounted lunch options
* A high\-performance, collaborative environment, operating at pace on cutting\-edge technologies.
* Career plan. Opportunity to learn and teach.
*As an equal opportunity employer, Multiverse Computing is committed to building an inclusive workplace. The company welcomes people from all different backgrounds, including age, citizenship, ethnic and racial origins, gender identities, individuals with disabilities, marital status, religions and ideologies, and sexual orientations to apply.*

title: Engineering Manager
description:
**About us**

Worley is a global company of energy, chemicals and resources experts headquartered in Australia. We partner with our customers to deliver projects and create value across the life of their assets. We specialize in consulting, engineering, procurement and construction across the project lifecycle, with services extending through to operations and decommissioning. Leveraging extensive experience and AI\-enabled delivery, we support customers in navigating complexity as they meet today's needs and transition to more sustainable solutions.


**JOB PURPOSE**  

The Engineering Manager will oversee and coordinate the development and implementation of FEED /EPC design and detailed engineering of the project.
  

Coordinate the development and implementation of feasibility and techno economic studies and in\-house multi\-disciplined engineering projects, relating to both new facilities and modifications to existing plant and systems. Generally, handles several projects and studies at a time, with full responsibility for delivering on time.
  

Controls project plans and budgets; ensures that effective supervision and project administration is carried out; provides information to management on project progress.


**KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES**  

  

**Job Specific Accountabilities**  

  

**Technical Assurance**

* Technical Integrity: Ensures the engineering team is verifying \& achieving technical Integrity of project deliverable(s) in compliance with:
	+ Legislation and Regulatory Commitments
	+ International Codes and Standards as listed in project documentation
	+ Basis of Design
	+ Contracts, annexures
* Technical Audits: Engage engineering team \&/or carry out technical audits (Discipline specific) to verify \& ensure compliance to specifications \&/or industry best practices.
* Technical/Design Reviews: Coordinate \& align all Project design reviews with the counterpart, identify/assign respective attendees from the engineering team, verify compliance with the approved respective procedure/Terms of reference, coordinate with other Packages engineering managers (as required) for involvement in reviews requiring input from their package(s) team.
* Review \& approval: review \& approval of critical “Multidiscipline” engineering documents, such as Basis of design, engineering execution plan, Philosophies, procedures \& specifications. Final approval of Engineering Technical Queries (post Discipline processing) \& request for information as issued by CONTRACTOR.
* Monitors CONTRACTOR’s overall compliance \& engineering quality, provides necessary feedback for observations, deviations \& areas of improvements (Preventive/Corrective actions) in the Project Quality system
* Provide input to project lesson learned register

**Performance Management**

* Competency management: Monitoring engineering team competencies, provide feedback about deficiencies (as applicable) to respective Team members, identifying areas of improvement, apply mentoring \& coaching as \& when required, communicate \& screen substitutes (if required).
* Productivity management: Monitoring engineering team productivity, provide feedback about deficiencies (as applicable) to respective Team members, clearly define productivity targets to team \& agree recovery plans (productivity improvement forecasts), apply mentoring \& coaching as \& when required, communicate \& screen substitutes (if required).
* KPI’s setting: Identify \& monitor a set of KPI’s for the function, KPI’s shall be inline/supporting the project/function KPI’s \& shall be Specific, Measurable, Attainable, Relevant, and Time\-Bound.
* Performance assessment: Carry out regular performance assessment with the team members based on the Set individual KPI’s \& the overall function performance, the outcome shall be fed back to the individuals, clearly identifying areas of improvement.

**Resources Management**

* Assess resource loading based on Project progress/forecasts reports \& as required request additional resources to support Project delivery.
* Manage/approve Engineering Team leaves \& ensures the leaves are cascaded to the overall Project leave planner, ensure handover’s \& deputations are in place during planned leaves.
* Manage/approve Engineering Team time writing \& time sheets.
* Process employees’ requests/ requirements for the assignment as per the respective Project procedures.
* Address conflicts \& disputes within the team \& align a resolution (as \& when required).

**Engineering Change Management**

* Approval of all Engineering Concession requests issued within the CONTRACT (post Discipline verification), prior to Project management approval, \- Ensure final Approval of Concession request from ADNOC Technical Authority (Engineering Support manager)
Monitoring \& ensuring (for the overall engineering activities) Engineering design change management is being carried out for all Project engineering changes with proper multidiscipline impact assessment \& the same is registered \& controlled (as \& when required).  
* 

**QUALIFICATIONS, EXPERIENCE, KNOWLEDGE \& SKILLS**  

  

**Minimum Qualification**

* Bachelor’s degree in engineering is a MUST
* Must be fluent in English language (Written \& Spoken).

**Minimum Experience \& Knowledge \& Skills**

* Minimum 20\+ working experience in the oil \& gas industry, with at least 5 years in a similar role in Mega Oil and Gas or petrochemical projects.
* Min of 5\+ years of GCC Projects experience, ADNOC Projects experience is required.
* Offshore Project Experience is required.
* Greenfield and brownfield project experience is required.
* Sound knowledge of industry governing codes \& standards,
* Excellent interpersonal and customer service skills.
* Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
* Excellent time management skills with a proven ability to meet deadlines.
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Strong supervisory and leadership skills.
Ability to prioritize tasks and to delegate them when appropriate.  
* 

**Moving forward together**

We want our people to be energized and empowered to drive sustainable impact. So, our focus is on a values\-inspired culture that unlocks brilliance through belonging, connection and innovation. We're building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace. Creating a space where everyone feels they belong, can be themselves, and are heard.


And we're not just talking about it; we're doing it. We're reskilling our people, leveraging transferable skills, and supporting the transition of our workforce to become experts in today's low carbon energy infrastructure and technology. Whatever your ambition, there's a path for you here.


And there's no barrier to your potential career success. Join us to broaden your horizons, explore diverse opportunities, and be part of delivering sustainable change.

title: Interface Manager
description:
**About us**

Worley is a global company of energy, chemicals and resources experts headquartered in Australia. We partner with our customers to deliver projects and create value across the life of their assets. We specialize in consulting, engineering, procurement and construction across the project lifecycle, with services extending through to operations and decommissioning. Leveraging extensive experience and AI\-enabled delivery, we support customers in navigating complexity as they meet today's needs and transition to more sustainable solutions.


**JOB PURPOSE**

Interface Manager is the role accountable for the overall Interface activities in the detail engineering phase at home office as well as Execution of the Project Interface activities at site which combines both technical and coordination aspects. The role holder acts as a principal point of contact who liaises with all internal and external stakeholders for various technical inputs and ensuring coordination among multiple parties / project packages that is required during the Project design and execution phase.


The Interface Manager shall lead and oversee all interface management activities, ensuring effective coordination, communication, monitoring, reporting, and control of interfaces among project stakeholders, while providing leadership and supervision to the Interface team


**KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES**

**Job Specific Accountabilities**

**External and Internal Interfaces**

* Interface Manager, Project Interfaces, is responsible for engineering coordination and Execution of assigned Interface requirements through close interaction with Internal and External parties / Stakeholders / End Users / EPC Contractors and will also identify, develop, coordinate, expedite and steward till closure of all Project Interfaces.
* Coordinates and establishes the regular interface meetings between EPC Contractor and Company.
* Leads the discussions and frequent coordination Operations, Material Handling Operations and Turn around Team for the execution of the interface activities.
* Provides regular coordination on Project Interfaces to Process/Commissioning Manager and accountable to provides monthly and weekly reports for progress and proactively identifying Interface issues.
* Coordinates and participates in various project related interface meetings and activities \& ensure the timely completion of the battery limit tie\-ins, tie\-ins related to scopes

**Technical Integration**

* Focal point and responsible to identify, define and execute the technical / physical internal and external interfaces through EPC phase by establishing Interface Point registers.
* Well monitoring and control of the IP Databases, managed for IP’s and IQ’s initiation/ correspondence / agreements to align with agreed Interdependency Schedule between EPC contractor and various Stakeholders.
* Actively participates in reviewing the Project Scope of work, various implementing strategies, execution plan, Constructing Strategies, etc.

**Design Alignment**

* Overall Responsibility for Interface Deliverables including but not limited to establishing and executing the interconnections with existing Borouge facilities and participating in Interface HAZOP for interface points in addition to other interface drawings and layouts as specified.
* Ensures that the HAZOP of battery limit (interface point) is carried out as per the requirements of the Project
* Organize Interface Meetings with internal and external parties for establishing and finalizing of the interface point register for multiple disciplines in a timely manner.

**Site Coordination**

* Closely working with FEED/EPC Contractor Interface team \& other stakeholders.
* Follow up of the required Interface related documentation and feedback FEED/EPC contractor and other stakeholders and sharing with Process/Commissioning Manager. Highlights the shortfalls and sought for support needed from the Process/Commissioning Manager on time in order to avoid the schedule delay.
* Focal point for identifying with Operations, Maintenance and Integrity Team, the key Interfaces between Project and Existing Facilities (Brownfield works) with particular focus to Interface/ tie\-ins during shutdown period activities.
* Focal point for maintaining the data base and reviewing all the pre\-commissioning and commissioning procedures of the various systems.

**Performance**

* Ensures the Pre\-commissioning \& Commissioning procedures are prepared on time and the reviews are done by the company on time.
* Productivity: Maintain the interface engineering documents review plan.
* Performance assessment: The three above parameters in addition to a set of individual KPI’s will be assessed on regular frequency (mid\-term \& final review) by the Process/Commissioning Manager and the outcome shall be fed back to the individual, clearly identifying areas of improvement (as applicable).

**Engineering Change Management**

* Review of Interface discipline specific Concession requests issued within the CONTRACT; validate the basis of the request \& its impact on technical integrity \& justification, prior to requesting endorsement from Process/Commissioning Manager.
* Monitoring \& ensuring that Interface discipline specific design change management is being carried out for all respective engineering changes with proper multidiscipline impact assessment \& the same is registered \& controlled (as \& when required).

**General**

* Maintain regular meetings with interface discipline counterpart for timely discipline activity coordination \& problems resolution as applicable.
* Communicate discipline specific updates/areas of concern on regular basis to the Process/Commissioning Manager.
* Address technical conflicts \& disputes with Counterpart (CONTRACTOR) in a professional \& systematic manner, relevant to governing specifications/codes \& actual situation.

**Operational Plans**

* Oversee and lead the interface executions and achievement to ensure achieving the desired outputs and performance by the team.
* Continuously monitor the pre\-commissioning/commissioning schedule of the project AND Procedures are developed by the EPC contractor and reviewed by company on time.
* Focus the development and ensure proper cascade of the status of the schedule to Process/Commissioning Manager in order to avoid any slippage.

### **QUALIFICATIONS, EXPERIENCE, KNOWLEDGE \& SKILLS**

**Minimum Qualification**

* Must be a 4 years bachelor’s degree in engineering
* Fluent in written and spoken English
* Proven skills and experience in brown field and green field projects.

**Minimum Experience \& Knowledge \& Skills**

* A bachelor’s degree in any/relevant Engineering or equivalent from an accredited university with minimum twenty (20\+) years of relevant experience in the Oil \& Gas or Petrochemical industry, including minimum (5\) years (preferred) in GCC region.
* Must have at least five (5\) years of experience in similar position for international Oil \& Gas or Petrochemical projects.
* Experience of working with Multiple packages for FEED and EPC Projects
* 5\+ Experience in ADNOC project is Required
* Offshore and Brown field Project Experience is Mandatory
* Experience in Project execution and knowledge of engineering standards is preferred.
* Promote team working and productive operating climate with a multi\-cultural, multi\-national workforce.
* Excellent time management skills with a proven ability to meet deadlines.
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills.

**Moving forward together**

We want our people to be energized and empowered to drive sustainable impact. So, our focus is on a values\-inspired culture that unlocks brilliance through belonging, connection and innovation. We're building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace. Creating a space where everyone feels they belong, can be themselves, and are heard.


And we're not just talking about it; we're doing it. We're reskilling our people, leveraging transferable skills, and supporting the transition of our workforce to become experts in today's low carbon energy infrastructure and technology. Whatever your ambition, there's a path for you here.


And there's no barrier to your potential career success. Join us to broaden your horizons, explore diverse opportunities, and be part of delivering sustainable change.

title: Sr. Manager Project Controls
description:
**About us**

Worley is a global company of energy, chemicals and resources experts headquartered in Australia. We partner with our customers to deliver projects and create value across the life of their assets. We specialize in consulting, engineering, procurement and construction across the project lifecycle, with services extending through to operations and decommissioning. Leveraging extensive experience and AI\-enabled delivery, we support customers in navigating complexity as they meet today's needs and transition to more sustainable solutions.


**JOB PURPOSE**  

A Sr. Project control Manager to Perform the project control function including project planning, cost control for the Project as per Project Execution Plan working closely with Project Manager.
  

The Sr.Project Controls Manager shall oversee all project control activities and provide leadership, guidance, and supervision to all Project Controls team members to ensure effective project planning, monitoring, reporting, and control to the project
  

Analyse and perform project planning, scheduling and monitoring for the Project to ensure completion of Project to the required safety, quality, schedule and cost.
  

  

**KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES**  

**Job Specific Accountabilities**  

**Project Control**

* Ensure baseline schedule follow\-up and monitoring, performance reporting, baseline change control assessment, risk management, earned value management guidance and tools.
* Advice and consult on the application of effective planning techniques and controls (earned value, cost and schedule variance, estimates at completion, critical path analysis).
* Support program integration, governance, formats, plan optimization, and project controls best practices.
* Support and advise on the definition of Project Controls and Earned Value Management policies, procedures and the standards required to deliver projects.
* Involve actively in the development of integrated project schedule for project that reflect all interface milestones, availability and need dates of feedstock, offsite and utilities and activity durations from multiple contractors’ schedules and assess changes to the integrated schedule.
* Monitor and control program compliance in respect of the project contractual obligations.
* Ensure contract, procurement, planning and scheduling activities within the project is performed as per Company requirements.
* Support the PCM on all contractual and commercial issues to maintain company’s interests.
* Coordinate and attend internal/external project reviews and other meetings, as necessary.
* Keep up to date with and adhere to Company and project related procedures.
* Ensure deliverables are delivered and that these are flagged to the PCM.
* Generate weekly / monthly reports in line with the project and company reporting calendar, provide details on progress, actuals, forecast and explanation of variances.
* Ensure that project controls processes are consistently applied across the project.
* Manage governance to ensure robust cost and schedule performance for the agreed Scope of Work across integrated programs managed across multiple parties.
* Support and advise on the definition of Project Controls and Management policies, procedures and the standards required to deliver projects.
* Work closely with the Senior Project Manager and other discipline Mangers to maximize the potential of the project.
* Manage and guide the review of Project Baseline Schedule to ensure compliance with the Contract Schedule requirements and the overall Project schedule.
* Instruct various reviews/analyses of the Project Schedule and the generation of special reports highlighting areas of slippage, concerns and forecasts.
* Manage and guide the process of Progress Certification and Invoice Payment under the Contract.
* Ensure appropriate certificates are generated and maintained for the Project denoting approval of various Milestones and Completion Activities.

**Internal Control**

* Develop and improve the project control procedures, systems and tools. Continuously work with the Project Management Teams to seek improvement opportunities and incorporate them in the standard ways of Project Controls.
* Be an active advocate of the project controls principles for better management of project operations.
* and aspects in enriching the desired culture through his / her internal and external communications.

**QUALIFICATIONS, EXPERIENCE, KNOWLEDGE \& SKILLS**  

**Minimum Qualification**

* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering from a reputable and recognized university.
* 20\+ years of project experience, working in the oil \& gas industry with previous knowledge of preparing and implementing project plans, schedules and cost control.
* A bachelor’s degree in any Engineering or other relevant legal or commercial from an accredited university with a minimum Twenty (20\+) years of contract experience in the Oil \& Gas or Petrochemical industry, including minimum five (5\) years (preferred) in GCC region.
* Must have at least five (5\) years of experience in similar position in mega Oil \& Gas or Petrochemical projects.
* Senior Project Control Manager will manage, as per their specialty, at least three (3\) EPC mega projects (minimum 1 Billion US$ each) or at least one mega project with value more than 5 Billion US$.
* Extensive Experience in Offshore Project is required.
* ADNOC project experience is required.
* PMC experience is required.

**Professional Certifications**

* Preferred but not mandatory to have PMP, CCP.

**TECHNICAL COMPETENCIES**

* Project control (Planning, scheduling, monitoring and control) skills.
* Full understanding of schedule, cost and risk analysis tools and techniques.
* Technical understanding and proficient knowledge related to Oil \& Gas business.
* Planning and implementation skills.
Detailed knowledge of principles, policies, procedures, and internal processes pertaining to project control transactions.  
* 

**Moving forward together**

We want our people to be energized and empowered to drive sustainable impact. So, our focus is on a values\-inspired culture that unlocks brilliance through belonging, connection and innovation. We're building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace. Creating a space where everyone feels they belong, can be themselves, and are heard.


And we're not just talking about it; we're doing it. We're reskilling our people, leveraging transferable skills, and supporting the transition of our workforce to become experts in today's low carbon energy infrastructure and technology. Whatever your ambition, there's a path for you here.


And there's no barrier to your potential career success. Join us to broaden your horizons, explore diverse opportunities, and be part of delivering sustainable change.

title: Solution Architect - Domain: Risk & Resilience
description:
Do you want to make a real impact on the security and resilience of a global technology company?


At Sandvik, we're strengthening our ability to manage risk, protect critical operations and meet evolving regulatory requirements. As a Solution Architect, you'll play a key role in shaping the future of Risk \& Resilience through one of our strategic enterprise platforms.

  

This is an opportunity to design solutions that enable secure, compliant and resilient operations across a global industrial technology organization.

 **Why Sandvik?**


At Sandvik, we're shaping the future of enterprise technology. As a Solution Architect for Risk \& Resilience, you'll play a key role in that journey—designing scalable, intelligent and sustainable solutions that support thousands of users across our global organization.

  

You'll influence strategic decisions, challenge conventional thinking and help define the architectural direction of a business\-critical platform. By driving best practices and architectural standards, you'll ensure our solutions remain robust, adaptable and ready for the future. You’ll join a focused, expert team working at the core of our digital landscape.

 **About the job**


As the solution architect for this domain, you’ll be the trusted technical authority for Risk \& Resilience within our ServiceNow platform. The Risk \& Resilience domain contains several capabilities related to Governance, Risk and Compliance. You’ll shape the technical direction of the domain while ensuring every solution aligns with architectural standards, platform strategy and long\-term business objectives.

  

You operate within the Platform Architecture \& Governance team, translating validated business needs into scalable, secure, and sustainable technical solutions while safeguarding platform integrity.

  

Your decisions directly influence platform stability, scalability, cost efficiency, automation levels and user experience.

  

Job responsibilities

* Define target architectures and architectural roadmap for your domain
* Influence platform standards and architectural principles
* Make strategic technology decisions that balance capability, maintainability and cost
* Champion an OOTB\-first mindset and challenge unnecessary customization
* Lead platform health, upgrade readiness and technical debt initiatives
* Guide engineers and implementation partners with architectural leadership and mentorship
* Help balance security, compliance, business value and platform maintainability
* Play a key role in preparing the platform for future innovations, including AI\-driven capabilities and automation.
 **Location**  

This role is based in Sandviken or at World Trade Center in Stockholm.

 **Your profile**


You combine a passion for architecture with a strong interest in the business outcomes technology enables.

  

Your background includes:

* Extensive experience designing enterprise\-scale solutions within ServiceNow or similar platforms
* Strong knowledge of Risk, Security or Resilience\-related ServiceNow capabilities
* A proven track record of driving platform transformation and reducing technical debt
* Strong understanding of platform governance, quality controls and lifecycle management
* Ability to contribute to cost estimations and articulate technical and commercial impacts
* Relevant academic background or equivalent experience in IT operations or service management
* ServiceNow certifications such as CSA and CIS (CTA or clear path to CTA is a plus)
  

You’re comfortable engaging with stakeholders across the organization and translating complex needs into clear technical direction.

  

You bring a collaborative mindset and communicate confidently with both technical and non\-technical audiences. Balancing detail with a strategic view comes naturally, and you’re driven to improve quality while ensuring long\-term sustainability.

 **Our culture**


At Sandvik, we’re tech driven, innovative and entrepreneurial. We believe that success is a team effort so we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where people can be themselves and reach their full potential. So, we invest in supporting each other, learning together and celebrating our differences. Visit our stories hub, LinkedIn or Facebook to get to know us further.

 **Contact information**


For further information about this position, please contact Jesper Sandlund, hiring manager, jesper.sandlund@sandvik.com.

  

We’ve already decided on which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to use and respectfully decline any additional contacts in that matter.

  

Union contacts – Sweden

* Malena Rackner, Unionen, \+46 (0\)70 242 33 90
* Tord Engström, Akademikerföreningen, \+46 (0\)76 106 62 87
* Peter Olsson\-Andrée, Ledarna, \+46 (0\)70 222 48 55
  

Recruitment Specialist: Lotta Amnebjer Nordqvist

 **How to apply**


Send your application no later than August 6, 2026\. Click apply and include your resume and cover letter in English. Please note that we don’t accept applications by e\-mail. Job ID: R0094544\.

  

We aim for an open and fair recruitment process and use different tools to ensure an objective assessment. Later in the process, you may be invited to complete a personality and logic test.

  

To learn more about our recruitment process, please visit our career site or contact HR Services at hrservices.sweden@sandvik.com.  



*Sandvik is a global, industrial technology group providing solutions that enhance productivity,**profitability**and sustainability for the manufacturing,**mining**and infrastructure industries. We are at the forefront of digitalization and focus on**optimizing**our customers’ processes. Our world\-leading offering includes equipment, tools, services and digital solutions for machining, mining, rock**excavation**and rock processing. In**2025**the Group had approximately**42,000 employees**and revenues of about SEK 121 billion in more than 150 countries.*

title: Infrastructure/Cloud Architecture
description:
**Who We Are**

At Kyndryl, we run and reimagine the mission\-critical technology systems that drive advantage for the world’s leading businesses. We are at the heart of progress; with proven expertise and a continuous flow of AI\-powered insight, enabling smarter decisions, faster innovation, and a lasting competitive edge. For our people—Kyndryls—that means doing purposeful work that powers human progress. Join us and experience a flexible, supportive environment where your well\-being is prioritized and your potential can thrive.  



  

**The Role**

As an Infrastructure/Cloud Architecture professional at Kyndryl, this role designs and guides cloud, Linux platform, container, and edge infrastructure architecture for a Kyndryl client’s software engineering and product platform environment. This is a software\-platform architecture role focused on cloud\-edge engineering, Linux platforms, Kubernetes, containers, DevOps integration, and platform readiness rather than traditional server administration or data\-center operations. Key responsibilities include designing cloud and platform architecture supporting engineering workloads, Linux platforms, containerized services, Kubernetes environments, DevOps pipelines, and product integration needs; supporting cloud implementation workstreams involving cloud architects, senior cloud engineers, platform engineers, DevOps engineers, technical leads, delivery leads, and project managers; providing guidance for scalability, reliability, automation, platform integration, infrastructure dependencies, operational readiness, and engineering enablement; collaborating with software, DevOps, testing, technical support, and delivery teams; and working within customer\-provided cloud platforms, infrastructure, environments, toolchains, licenses, access models, and governance frameworks.  

  

**Who You Are**

You’re good at what you do and possess the required experience to prove it. However, equally as important – you have a growth mindset; keen to drive your own personal and professional development. You are customer\-focused – someone who prioritizes customer success in their work. And finally, you’re open and borderless – naturally inclusive in how you work with others. Required Technical and Professional Experience • Solid understanding of Data Center mainly Systems, Storage, Networking \& Security, Application \& Databases • Understanding of Systems and Solutions Architecture • Hands\-on experience \& learning with Data Center technologies, Cloud Software, and interoperability Preferred Technical and Professional Experience • MS Project or other Project Management software • Excellent written and verbal communication skills • Ability to interact confidently with stakeholders of all levels  

  

**Being You**

The “Kyn” in Kyndryl means kinship, which represents the strong bonds we have with each other, our customers and our communities. We focus on ensuring all Kyndryls feel included and we welcome people of all cultures, backgrounds, and experiences. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply. We believe in growth, and we’re excited to see what you can bring. At Kyndryl, employee feedback has told us that our number one driver of employee engagement is belonging. That sense of belonging — being a valued, respected, trusted member of the team — is fundamental to our culture and fueling great experiences for our customers. This dedication to welcoming everyone into our company means that Kyndryl gives you the ability to thrive and contribute to our culture of empathy and shared success. That’s The Kyndryl Way.  



  

**What You Can Expect**

Your career with us isn’t just a job—it’s an adventure with purpose. We offer a dynamic, hybrid\-friendly culture that supports your well\-being and empowers you to grow. Our Be Well programs are thoughtfully designed to support your financial, mental, physical, and social health—because we know that when you feel your best, you do your best.
  

  

From your very first day, you’ll dive into impactful work that powers the systems our customers rely on every day. You won’t just contribute—you’ll make a difference, tackling meaningful projects that sharpen your skills and fuel your growth.
  

  

We’re here to champion your journey. With powerful tools to chart your career path, personalized development goals aligned with your ambitions, and continuous feedback to keep you inspired and on track, you’ll have everything you need to thrive and evolve. You’ll develop in\-demand skills to grow your career and achieve your ambitions with access to cutting\-edge learning opportunities—from certifications with Microsoft, Google, and Amazon to coaching and hands\-on experiences. And through it all, you’ll be part of a culture that values empathy, restless learning, and a devotion to shared success.
  

  

We want you to thrive here—and we’re committed to helping you do just that. Ready to make an impact? Join us and help shape what’s next.


**Get Referred!**

If you know someone that works at Kyndryl, when asked ‘How Did You Hear About Us’ during the application process, select ‘Employee Referral’ and enter your contact's Kyndryl email address.

title: Technical Support
description:

At Kyndryl, we run and reimagine the mission\-critical technology systems that drive advantage for the world’s leading businesses. The role provides Level 1 and/or Level 2 support, including case triage, diagnostics, issue reproduction, technical investigation, root\-cause support, workaround assistance, case documentation, and escalation support within customer\-defined support systems and communication standards. Key responsibilities include Level 1 case intake, triage, diagnostic information collection, known\-solution guidance, next\-step communication, case status tracking, and issue reproduction; Level 2 advanced troubleshooting, technical investigation, root\-cause support, workaround development, and deeper issue reproduction across software, platform, and integration layers; supporting complex customer environments including lab, integration, platform, and certification\-related contexts; documenting solutions, contributing to knowledge resources, mentoring support engineers, and improving processes; escalating defects, product issues, security concerns, or certification/compliance questions through approved channels; and operating within defined role boundaries without making architectural, compliance, or security posture commitments.
